page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.2xlarge is an Arm64-based compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs with a single thread per core and is paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor, includes no local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves average multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench, alongside solid multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, single-core metrics are weaker, with Geekbench single-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25% tier. Cryptographic performance is also limited, as shown by a weak PassMark CPU encryption score. Memory bandwidth remains stable, reaching 568.26 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 92.39 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated Arm64 architecture offers high resource density for parallelized workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, a modest 2 Gbps network baseline, and lower single-threaded efficiency. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations that can leverage the dedicated physical cores of the Ampere Altra processor.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6r.2xlarge is an Arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14295.27 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6139.0, though single-core and encryption performance fall into the lower tier. The dedicated, single-threaded core design offers qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized tasks. This instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
